title: Mason

rationales.taskAutomation: Bricklaying and block-setting are manual craft requiring physical precision that has no automated substitute at typical job-site scale.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic bricklaying exists only in limited pilot deployments, not shipped production tools.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of any AI or robotic tooling in masonry is rare, concentrated in a handful of large commercial contractors piloting robotic layers.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can generate a material takeoff from a wall plan; it cannot lay a course of brick to line and level.

tasksGrowing: Custom and historic masonry restoration requiring craft judgment
Cross-training toward larger commercial masonry projects
salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 47-2021, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Digital takeoff and estimating software literacy

insight: Masonry is among the lowest-risk trades: robotic bricklaying remains a pilot-stage novelty, and the craft's precision work stays entirely manual.
